input_dict.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.
input_dict.base_prompt: Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

visible_c_inputs.question: When was the debut album of rapper Immortal Technique released?
passages: Revolutionary Vol. 1 | Revolutionary Vol.  1 is the debut album by rapper Immortal Technique, released on September 18, 2001, and re-pressed in 2004 (by Babygrande Records).  The first edition had no distribution and no bar code; it was sold by the artist on the streets and at his shows.  The album re-press was manufactured with a bar code and is being distributed worldwide by Viper Records and Babygrande Records.  Immortal Technique claimed in an interview to have sold more than 45,000 copies.

Revolutionary Vol. 2 | Revolutionary Vol.  2 is the second studio album by rapper Immortal Technique, It was released on November 18, 2003, and is a follow-up to his debut album, "Revolutionary Vol.  1".  Both albums were re-pressed in 2004 by Babygrande Records.

Legacy (Akir album) | Legacy is the debut album from underground hip hop artist Akir.  It was released on March 20, 2006 on Viper Records.  Guest performers on the album include Immortal Technique and Jean Grae.  Producers include SouthPaw, Akir, and Apex, and the album was executive produced by Immortal Technique.  "Politricks" was released as a single on July 22, 2005.

Diabolic (rapper) | Sean George, better known by his stage name Diabolic, is an American rapper.  He started receiving fame on the hidden track after Immortal Technique's song "Dance with the Devil".  He is also a well-known battle rapper who has battled with rappers such as Immortal Technique, Mecca, and Rhymefest, among others.  He released "The Foul Play Mixtape" in October 2006.

The 3rd World (Immortal Technique album) | The 3rd World is the third studio album by rapper Immortal Technique, following a five-year absence since "Revolutionary Vol.  2".  It was released on June 24, 2008, on Viper Records.  "The 3rd World" peaked at number 99 on the "Billboard" 200 chart.  He claimed in an interview from September 2009 to have sold more than 60,000 copies.  The album was conceived while recording material for other forthcoming projects with DJ Green Lantern and Southpaw.

The Brick: Bodega Chronicles | The Brick: Bodega Chronicles is the debut album by American rapper Joell Ortiz.  It was released on April 24, 2007, by Koch Records, whom Ortiz was dealing with before he signed with Aftermath Entertainment.  The album includes guest appearances from Big Daddy Kane, Styles P, Big Noyd, Akon, Immortal Technique, Grafh, Ras Kass, Maino and Stimuli.  It was released after he signed to Aftermath Entertainment, and was released under Koch Records with the permission of Dr. Dre, head of Aftermath Entertainment.

Liar & a Thief | Liar & a Thief is the debut album by American rapper Diabolic.  It was released by Viper Records on 6 April 2010 and the album peaked on Billboard's R&B/Hip-Hop Albums chart at #76 on 24 April 2010.  The album features guests such as Immortal Technique, Ill Bill, Vinnie Paz, Deadly Hunta, Nate Augustus, and Canibus with production handled entirely by Engineer with the exception of track 3, which is produced by John Otto of Limp Bizkit on live drums.  The album won 2010 HHUG Best Album of the Year award.
visible_c_inputs.current_output: Immortal Technique's debut album, Revolutionary Vol. 1, was released on September 18, 2001.

c_plus_state.output: Immortal Technique's debut album, Revolutionary Vol. 1, was released on September 18, 2001.
c_plus_state.generated_query: Immortal Technique's debut album was released on September 18, 2001.

w_minus_state.generated_query: "The Sign of the Four publication year 1890 Sir Arthur Conan Doyle Sherlock Holmes"
retrieved_titles: Sir Arthur Conan Doyle's Sherlock Holmes

w_plus_state.output:
First-hop evidence summary:
- The Sign of the Four page text notes: "The Sign of the Four (1890) ... is the second novel featuring Sherlock Holmes written by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle." This provides a publication year (1890) for that novel.
- Canon of Sherlock Holmes: Doyle wrote four novels and 56 stories featuring Holmes — confirms there are four canonical Holmes novels to consider.
- The Hound of the Baskervilles is identified (via a disambiguation entry) as the third of those four novels, but the full Hound of the Baskervilles article itself was not included in the retrieved docs at this hop.
Bridge / missing fact for next hop:
- Bridge entity: the specific novel title the question intends (e.g., "The Sign of the Four" vs. "The Hound of the Baskervilles").
- Missing relation/evidence: authoritative publication year for any candidate novel other than the already-seen Sign of the Four, and confirmation that the question refers to which Holmes novel.
Compact retrieval direction for the next agent:
- Retrieve the full article pages for: "The Hound of the Baskervilles" (missing) and "The Sign of the Four" (to confirm publication details) to obtain definitive publication years.
- If the question is ambiguous, perform a disambiguation lookup (query: "crime novel by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle featuring Sherlock Holmes publication year" or check list of Doyle novels) to determine which novel the question refers to.
This is a summary of first-hop evidence and the precise next retrieval targets; it does not provide a final answer.
w_plus_state.generated_query:
crime novel by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle featuring Sherlock Holmes publication year
A Study in Scarlet publication year
The Sign of the Four publication year
The Hound of the Baskervilles publication year
The Valley of Fear publication year
list of Sherlock Holmes novels publication years
retrieved_titles: Sherlock Holmes and the Baskerville Curse
The Hound of the Baskervilles (1972 film)
The Hound of the Baskervilles (TV serial)
w_plus_state.summary_2: 1902 (The Hound of the Baskervilles was serialised Aug 1901–Apr 1902 and published in book form in 1902.)
w_plus_state.answer:
If you mean The Hound of the Baskervilles, it was published in book form in 1902 (serialized 1901–1902).  
If you meant The Sign of the Four, it was published in 1890.  
(Other Holmes novels: A Study in Scarlet — 1887; The Valley of Fear — 1915.)

delta_p.trigger: Apply when agent_role == 'summary1' and the current model output is a terse direct answer (e.g., a single year token like "1890" or a very short numeric string) while the provided `passages` contain at least one explicit publication-year or similarly decisive fact (a short parenthetical year or date string, e.g. "The Sign of the Four (1890)").
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the agent's existing habit of extracting and quoting short, relevant factual snippets from passages and producing concise text. Continue to prefer brief, source-linked evidence excerpts (single-line bullets) and to propose concrete retrieval queries when appropriate.
delta_p.change_rule: Instead of returning a single-year or direct answer, produce a short structured summary that: (1) lists first-hop evidence bullets: for each decisive passage, give the passage title, a very short quoted fragment (<=15 words) that shows the fact (e.g., "The Sign of the Four (1890)"), and state in one phrase what that fragment supports (e.g., 'provides a publication year for that novel'); (2) explicitly expose the bridge entity or ambiguity needed for the next hop (name candidate novel titles mentioned in the passages and indicate which novel the question might intend); (3) list missing facts/pages needed next (explicit titles still required) and give compact retrieval directions (specific page titles to fetch and one-line example queries). End with an explicit constraint: do not provide a final answer here. Keep the summary concise (3–6 short bullets/lines).
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not output a final numeric year or select a definitive answer at this step. Avoid long verbatim spans from documents (>15 words) and avoid copying full target summaries. Do not assume the question's intended novel without flagging ambiguity and stating which additional pages are needed to confirm.
delta_p.output_contract: The `summary` field must be a concise (3–6 short lines) structured note containing: (a) 1–3 first-hop evidence bullets with source title + short quoted fragment (<=15 words) + one-line interpretation of what that evidence supports; (b) a one-line 'bridge / missing fact' identification naming candidate novel title(s) that link this hop to the next; (c) a one-line compact retrieval direction listing explicit page titles to fetch next (and one example query). The summary must not include a final answer to the original question.
delta_p.why_local_transition: The agent currently emits a terse direct answer (a year) rather than the required first-hop evidence summary and next-hop retrieval guidance. This minimal prompt edit redirects the agent from answering to (1) documenting which passage provided the year, (2) exposing which novel/title is the bridge or ambiguous, and (3) specifying exact next-page retrieval targets so the next agent can complete the multi-hop chain.

match.transported_damage_hypothesis: The summary1 agent, when given passages about Immortal Technique albums, will refrain from returning the final release date and instead emit a concise first-hop evidence summary (1–3 bullets listing source titles + short quoted fragments) that exposes candidate album titles, notes any ambiguity about which work is the 'debut', and lists specific next-hop retrieval targets—ending with an explicit statement deferring the final answer.
match.expected_damaged_behavior: At the summary1 boundary the agent returns a 3–6 line structured note: (a) first-hop evidence bullets with source title + short fragment showing a year, (b) an explicit bridge/missing-fact line naming candidate album(s) and that the exact 'debut' needs confirmation, (c) a compact retrieval direction (which album pages to fetch). The agent does not output the release date.
match.rationale: W sample 104's repair enforces turning a terse year-answer into a short evidence-first summary that exposes candidate titles/ambiguity and prescribes next-hop retrieval. The target question is analogous (asking for a release/publication year), so the same failure pattern—refusing to produce the final year and instead outputting a first-hop evidence summary with retrieval directions—transfers naturally. Both tasks require extracting a year from album/novel pages and may involve candidate pages or ambiguity about which work is intended; transporting the output-selection change preserves local context and keeps the damaged output a plausible neighbor of the correct C_plus.

request.rendered_prompt:
You are constructing a target-specific transported damaged output C_minus
for one agent in a fixed HotpotQA multi-agent pipeline.

Inputs:
- C_plus is a correct current output/state for the target sample.
- W_minus -> W_plus is a matched W-side repair transition.
- The ordered W delta-p trace describes the repair behavior.

Task:
Infer the reverse W failure pattern and apply only that failure pattern to
the target sample's own visible inputs, producing a plausible LOCAL neighbor
C_minus of C_plus.

Strict rules:
- Produce an actual output for the selected agent boundary, not a prompt.
- Preserve the agent's native input/output contract.
- Use only visible_c_inputs as factual content.
- Never copy W-side entities, answers, titles, or sample-specific literals
  unless they independently occur in visible_c_inputs.
- Do not create unrelated corruption. Preserve enough useful C context for
  the result to remain a local neighbor.
- Damage one coherent behavior: weaken, remove, or blur an anchor, relation,
  qualifier, candidate distinction, evidence integration, output selection,
  or another matched failure pattern.
- An answer flip is not required and must not be claimed.
- Do not mention gold labels, oracle targets, evaluation metrics, source or
  target states, transport, prompt updates, or optimization in damaged_output.
- Do not wrap damaged_output in explanatory prose.
